entries.reason: historical changelog entries document the prior project name; not public-facing branding
module rename tracked separately under epic #186; internal-only, not a public artifact surface

internal ACP adapter module importing from hermes_cli/hermes_constants and using the frozen HERMES_* env contract (e.g. hermes-setup auth method id); same module-rename scope as hermes_cli/*, tracked under epic #186 (issue #187 classification pass)
core entry point importing hermes_cli/hermes_constants/hermes_state internals and using the frozen HERMES_* env contract; not user-facing branding (issue #187 classification pass)
container build paths (/opt/hermes, HERMES_UID, the `hermes` unix user/process name) mirror the frozen HERMES_HOME/HERMES_* runtime contract; not user-facing branding (issue #187 classification pass)
compose service/volume/env names mirror the frozen HERMES_HOME/HERMES_* runtime contract used by Dockerfile; not user-facing branding (issue #187 classification pass)
ignore patterns reference internal .hermes/hermes_cli paths, not user-facing branding (issue #187 classification pass)
HERMES_* env var names in the example env file mirror the frozen runtime contract; not user-facing branding (issue #187 classification pass)

remaining occurrences after the issue #200/#205/#187 branding pass are deliberate ADR-0010 role terminology ('Hermes reasons and coordinates' — the LLM's reasoning role, distinct from the Simplicio CLI execution role) and references to the frozen HERMES_*/hermes_cli internal contract; not unaddressed product branding

[project.scripts] deliberately documents `hermes`/`hermes-agent`/`hermes-acp` as deprecated compatibility aliases for the canonical `simplicio-agent`/`simplicio-agent-acp` (see AGENTS.md 'Product identity'); py-modules/[tool.setuptools] include the frozen hermes_* internal module names; other comments reference internal modules or CLI examples, not unaddressed branding (issue #187 classification pass)

remaining occurrences are the deliberate Hermes→Simplicio attribution/comparison section (upstream credit, benchmark table, MIT license notice) and documented `hermes` deprecated-alias notes; not unaddressed branding (issue #187 classification pass)

`hermes-fast`/`hermes_fast` is the internal Rust accelerator crate/module name consumed by agent._hermes_fast as a fallback-guarded internal module, not a public product name; module rename tracked under epic #186 (issue #187 classification pass)
internal design/planning docs (not shipped) referencing the frozen HERMES_HOME/hermes_cli contract and internal diagram labels; not user-facing branding (issue #187 classification pass)
internal dev/dataset-generation scripts referencing the frozen ~/.hermes/ path contract, not user-facing branding (issue #187 classification pass)
internal git hooks reading HERMES_HOME as the documented fallback for SIMPLICIO_AGENT_HOME during the identity migration (issue #117); not user-facing branding (issue #187 classification pass)
lint-ignore comments reference the frozen HERMES_UID/hermes container user contract (matches Dockerfile), not user-facing branding (issue #187 classification pass)
comment references the internal hermes_cli/plugins.py module, not user-facing branding (issue #187 classification pass)
test suite mirrors the hermes_cli module path pending #186 module rename
frozen archive of pre-fork upstream source, retained for reference — not shipped in any artifact

wheel build metadata (METADATA/RECORD/entry_points.txt/WHEEL/top_level.txt) is generated verbatim from the already-allowlisted pyproject.toml [project] table and the source file list, not a new leak surface — see tools/rename_guard/artifact_scan.py (issue #194)
sdist build metadata is generated verbatim from the already-allowlisted pyproject.toml [project] table, not a new leak surface — see tools/rename_guard/artifact_scan.py (issue #194)
setuptools egg-info is generated verbatim from the already-allowlisted pyproject.toml [project] table, not a new leak surface — see tools/rename_guard/artifact_scan.py (issue #194)

capability-contract skill catalog lists installed skill directories/descriptions (issue #186 epic) that legitimately quote legacy 'hermes-agent' skill names and product history; not a new branding regression
rename-guard status report for issue #187 that quotes internal paths (hermes_cli/*, hermes_constants.py) verbatim for auditing purposes; not a branding regression
identity scanner implementation must contain the legacy matcher and schema terms it detects; scanner is not a product identity surface
focused identity scanner tests intentionally exercise legacy references
release contract test intentionally proves legacy runtime identities are rejected
machine-readable identity inventory intentionally records temporary legacy aliases
migration ADR documents the bounded legacy scanner contract
